data.title: U.S. Senate to Question CFTC Chair Nominee on Cryptocurrency Regulation
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. senators are set to question Michael Selig on Wednesday regarding his reform plans on cryptocurrency regulation and election prediction markets. Selig, nominated by U.S. President Donald Trump as the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) chair, currently serves as the chief legal advisor for the SEC's cryptocurrency task force and advises Paul Atkins, the Republican SEC chairman appointed by Trump.

If Congress passes the CLARITY Act, the CFTC, responsible for regulating the commodity derivatives market, will play a more active role in the cryptocurrency industry. Selig is expected to be questioned about the CLARITY Act and how the CFTC plans to collaborate with the SEC, as well as regulate markets that allow Americans to bet on election outcomes and other events.

Traditionally, the CFTC is led by five bipartisan commissioners nominated by the president. Currently, the agency has only one commissioner, Republican Caroline Pham, who is serving as acting chair. Pham has stated that she will leave the agency once Trump's nominee for chair is confirmed.

data.title: TopNod Wallet integrates the 1inch Swap API to enhance the RWA asset handling experience.
data.description: TopNod has announced the official integration of the 1inch Swap API, introducing an RWA asset trading experience. Users can now directly purchase on-chain assets such as NVDAon and TSLAon using cryptocurrency. This integration aims to simplify users' interaction with real-world asset markets. TopNod Wallet has also launched a new user reward program totaling 100,000 USDC, which users can earn by completing tasks.

data.title: Hong Kong plans to extend tax exemptions to digital assets, with a bill to be submitted next year.
data.description: In a recent interview, Hong Kong's Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Christopher Hui, revealed that Hong Kong is continuously optimizing its tax exemption policy and plans to further expand the existing tax exemption scope from family offices and funds to emerging product categories such as private lending, carbon credits, and digital assets. A bill will be submitted to the Legislative Council next year. (21st Century Business Herald)

data.description:
The crypto market is up today. BTC increased to $91,381, and ETH is up to $3,061. “BTC at $250,000 by the end of this year may seem more aspirational than realistic. ”

data.title: Ondo Global Markets Secures Approval to Offer Tokenized U.S. Stocks in Europe
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Ondo Global Markets has received approval from the Financial Market Authority (FMA) to offer tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs across 30 European countries. This development allows the company to expand its financial services in the region, providing investors with access to a broader range of investment opportunities through tokenization.

data.title: Ondo Global Markets has been approved to offer tokenized US stocks and ETFs in 30 European countries.
data.description: Ondo Global Markets has received FMA approval to offer tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs in 30 European countries. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: The US Senate will question CFTC chairman candidates on crypto regulation issues.
data.description: U.S. senators are scheduled to question Michael Selig on Wednesday about his reform plans on hot-button issues such as cryptocurrency regulation and election prediction. Selig is President Trump's nominee for chairman of the CFTC. Selig is currently the chief legal counsel for the SEC's cryptocurrency task force and an advisor to Paul Atkins, the Republican chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission appointed by Trump. If Congress passes a bill called the CLARITY Act, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, which regulates the commodity derivatives market, will also play a more active role in the regulation of the cryptocurrency industry. Selig is expected to be questioned about the CLARITY Act, as well as how the CFTC will cooperate with the SEC and how it will regulate markets that allow Americans to bet on election results and other events. The CFTC is traditionally led by five bipartisan commissioners nominated by the president. Currently, the agency has only one commissioner, Republican Caroline Pham, who also serves as acting chair. Pham has stated that she will leave the agency once Trump's nominee for chairman is confirmed. (Reuters)

data.title: Deribit: $140,000 worth of BTC call options has been overtaken, with open interest now below $85,000 for put options.
data.description: Deribit, a derivatives trading platform, posted on its X platform that the $140,000 Bitcoin call options have lost their leading position, with current open interest at approximately $1.63 billion. In contrast, the $85,000 put options have an open interest of $2.05 billion. This reflects a shift in market sentiment from extremely bullish to extreme downside protection after Bitcoin fell more than 25% to $91,000.

data.title: Wintermute: Traders should be allowed to conduct on-chain settlements directly; DeFi proprietary trading should not require registration.
data.description: Crypto market maker Wintermute posted on the X platform that it has followed up with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Cryptocurrency Working Group and raised two key points: 1. Regulated dealers should be authorized to develop their own clearing and settlement procedures for tokenized securities without triggering the rules applicable to broker-dealers providing clearing services to clients. This would allow dealers to settle directly with counterparties on-chain and adopt customized risk management standards; 2. Proprietary trading and liquidity provision on DeFi protocols should not trigger dealer registration. If a company only trades for its own account and does not interact with clients, the activity falls under the long-standing "trader exemption" provision.

data.title: Gate launched its 24th Contract Points Airdrop, with a single account earning nearly $29.
data.description: According to on-chain data, the highest price of WCT in the 24-hour period of the Gate.com Contract Points (GMT) airdrop was $0.1702, with a current market capitalization of approximately $31.114 million. The single-account profit for this airdrop was nearly $29. It is understood that this airdrop was divided into two prize pools: users holding ≥ 40 Gate.com Contract Points could receive a 100 USDT position trial voucher, consuming 20 Gate.com Contract Points; those holding ≥ 120 Gate.com Contract Points could receive 170 WCT, consuming 15 Gate.com Contract Points airdrop for single users, offering 60 SPX and a 100 USDT position trial voucher, will begin on November 20th at 19:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin price remains above $90,000, retail selling intensifies.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently hovering in a fragile area just above $90,000, with retail selling, large outflows from ETFs, and rising downside hedging pressures continuing to determine the market structure until the end of the year. Analysts say the current trading record reflects an unbalanced pattern of fund flows—short-term holders and Wall Street investors are selling heavily, while long-term holders, or investors often referred to as "whales," are steadily accumulating. Timothy Misir, head of research at BRN, points out that Bitcoin is at a "crossroads," with large holders continuing to increase their holdings while retail investors and short-term buyers continue to suffer huge losses. Recently, approximately 31,800 bitcoins were transferred to exchanges at a loss, while the number of wallets holding more than 1,000 bitcoins increased by 2.2%, the highest growth rate in four months. (The Block)

data.title: A whale deposited $4.87 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a long position on ETH with 20x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $4.87 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened long ETH positions with 20x leverage in two wallets. In addition, the whale spent $387,861 USDC to buy 10,043 HYPE tokens at $38.60 each and then staked them.

data.title: Coinbase taps Kalshi to develop prediction markets platform
data.description:
Coinbase plans a prediction markets platform using Kalshi’s regulated system. Users can trade USDC or USD across sports, politics, and tech events. Move aligns with Coinbase’s goal to become an “everything exchange. ” Coinbase is preparing to enter the rapidly growing prediction markets sector, leveraging the regulated infrastructure of Kalshi to build its own platform

data.title: Revolut Partners with Polygon for Stablecoin Transactions in Europe
data.description:
According to PANews, on November 18, European neobank Revolut announced a partnership with Polygon Labs, the Ethereum Layer 2 scaling network, to utilize Polygon as its preferred technology stack for stablecoin transfers, payments, and transactions. Customers in the UK and the European Economic Area (non-EU countries) can use the Revolut app and Polygon blockchain to remit cryptocurrencies such as USDC, USDT, and POL. This integration aligns with the EU's MiCA regulatory framework and considers the regulatory requirements for stablecoins like USDT.

The press release states that Revolut will enable Polygon network support for stablecoin transfers in its app by early December 2024. As of November this year, transactions processed through the Revolut app using Polygon have exceeded $690 million. The integration offers a seamless Web3 experience within the Revolut app, featuring core functionalities such as:

- Instant cross-border transfers of stablecoins USDC, USDT, and POL.

- Crypto card payments within the Revolut app, allowing users to pay for everyday expenses using stablecoins.

- Support for POL tokens, enabling users to stake or trade POL directly through the Revolut app, simplifying access to the Polygon ecosystem without external wallets.

- Direct fiat currency exchange within Revolut, lowering entry barriers and promoting stablecoin circulation, with over $3.6 billion in stablecoin supply on Polygon.

Revolut serves over 65 million users across 38 countries, including 14 million crypto users. Since 2017, the bank has offered cryptocurrency trading services in certain regions, but due to regulatory uncertainties, this feature was discontinued for U.S. users in 2023.

Last month, the company obtained a MiCA license in Cyprus, potentially paving the way for launching its own stablecoin. In June, insiders revealed that Revolut was considering launching its own stablecoin, following the expansion of its cryptocurrency services to over 30 countries with the introduction of Revolut X a year ago.

The integration with Polygon follows the network's significant payment upgrade, named Rio, which enhances speed, efficiency, and reliability, achieving nearly instant settlement of up to 5,000 transactions per second, ensuring high throughput and low latency.

In addition to Revolut, other institutions like Stripe, Mastercard, Flutterwave, and Reliance Jio have chosen Polygon as their cryptocurrency payment channel. Revolut's adoption of Polygon reflects a broader trend as businesses seek to reduce transaction costs and improve service efficiency. The long-term application of these systems will depend on their stability, compliance, and user acceptance of volatile digital assets.
